examples: Unless the tests are ineptly designed, removing tests will just remove power.
In the cockpit she found the droid fiddling ineptly at the controls. "Leave that alone, " she snapped. "I'll do it. "
And a lot of today's campaigns are based on optimum positioning but are totally ineffective-because they are dull, or badly constructed, or ineptly written.
And now the German finance minister, Theo Waigel, has ineptly thrown the whole project into crisis.
In 1992 he tried ineptly to clarify the question of the royal succession.
